questions.text: If the sum of two angles is 90°, they are called:
questions.explanation: Complementary angles are a pair of angles whose sum is exactly 90 degrees.

questions.text: What is the supplement of an angle of 70°?
Angles which have a common vertex and a common arm but no common interior points are:
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles share a vertex and an arm, placed side-by-side.

questions.text: When two lines intersect, the vertically opposite angles are always:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles formed by two intersecting lines are equal in measure.

questions.text: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points is called a:
questions.explanation: A transversal line cuts through other lines, creating various angle relationships.

questions.text: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, each pair of alternate interior angles is:
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when the transversal intersects parallel lines.

questions.text: A linear pair of angles is always:
questions.explanation: Angles in a linear pair form a straight line, so their sum is 180° (supplementary).

questions.text: Which of the following cannot be a pair of complementary angles?
questions.explanation: The sum of complementary angles must be 90°. 90° + 90° = 180°.

questions.text: If one angle of a linear pair is acute, the other angle will be:
questions.explanation: Since the sum is 180°, if one is less than 90°, the other must be greater than 90°.

questions.text: Two lines in a plane which do not meet at any point are called:
questions.explanation: Parallel lines maintain the same distance apart and never intersect.
